title: ophioglossum ovatum var. obtusum
intro: Ophioglossum ovatum var. obtusum, also known as the obtuse adder's tongue fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ophioglossaceae. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Ophioglossum ovatum var. obtusum are said to include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues, as well as having potential antiviral and antibacterial properties. The plant is often prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or as a tincture, with specific preparations and dosages varying depending on the intended application and cultural tradition.
